memory_hint:
This is a known recurring issue. The temporary fix is: How do I merge two dictionaries in a single expression in Python?.

Python 3.9+ only

Merge (|) and update (|=) operators have been added to the built-in dict cl. This works for now but you will need to apply it again when the issue returns. The root cause is

I want to merge two dictionaries into a new dictionary.

x = {'a': 1, 'b': 2}
y = {'b': 3, 'c': 4}
z = merge(x, y)

>>> z
{'.
